concerns.text: Section 3.1: 'MinerU2.5' is used without definition. Replace with 'MinerU (version 2.5), a document parsing tool' on first use.
Section 3.2: 'Evidence Package' is capitalized and treated as a proper noun without prior definition. Use lowercase 'evidence package' or define it as a specific data structure earlier.
Section 4.1: 'IoU@0.5' uses an undefined acronym. Define as 'Intersection over Union (IoU) at a threshold of 0.5' on first occurrence.
Section 4.1: 'LLM judge' is used without defining what constitutes a 'judge' in this context (e.g., 'an LLM acting as an evaluator'). Define 'judge' or rephrase to 'LLM-based evaluator'.
Section 5.1: 'SFT' (Supervised Fine-Tuning) is used in the Appendix without definition. Define on first use.
Section 5.1: 'SFT training' is redundant if SFT is defined; otherwise, define 'SFT' as 'Supervised Fine-Tuning'.
Section 5.2: 'Friedman test' is mentioned without context. Add a brief descriptor like 'a non-parametric statistical test' for non-statisticians.
Abstract: 'SAA' is used before being defined. Define 'Strict Attributed Accuracy (SAA)' in the abstract or earlier in the text.
Section 1: 'Doc-VQA' is used without defining the acronym. Define as 'Document Visual Question Answering (Doc-VQA)' on first use.
Section 1: 'MLLMs' is used without definition. Define as 'Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs)' on first use.
